meaning: n.官话,普通话,满清官吏,中国柑桔adj.官僚的,保守知识界的,(语言或文体)繁复而华美的,(中国式)紧身马褂的
examples: They no longer spoke Mandarin well enough to answer questions.
All visitors receive the free audio guide in Russian and Mandarin.
In Mandarin, The Forbidden City is called the Purple Forbidden City.
That could mean publishing restaurant reviews in Mandarin or finding new platforms for distribution.
Hotel management makes regular recruitment trips to China, and all staff are encouraged to learn Mandarin.
